input.content:
Drag the amber query, or focus it and press the arrow keys, to turn it toward a key.

q {Math.round(angle)}°

{#each keyGeo as k, i}
{k.name}
{scores[i].toFixed(2)}
{/each}

content.content: <tool_use_error>File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.</tool_use_error>

tool_use_result: Error: File has not been read yet. Read it first before writing to it.

file.content:
Turn q until it lines up with a key.

q = {queryAngle} deg

{#each scores as s (s.id)}
{s.id}
{fmt(s.score)}
{/each}

content.text:
The existing component at that path already implements the full spec correctly (single `role="slider"` drag/keyboard control, amber query vector, periwinkle keys at 20/70/110/160, lilac diverging score bars with mono readouts, cream on-object labels, reduced-motion guard, all-token colors, no props, SSR-safe). The iteration 1 failure was an infrastructure browser-launch crash, not a defect in this code, so no functional change was needed; I verified the file for correctness and left it in place.

DONE Verified/kept QkExplorer.svelte: amber draggable query vector (role="slider" on the diagram, keyboard +/-5 with Home/End, pointer drag anywhere in the >=220px hit area), four static periwinkle key vectors labeled k1..k4 at 20/70/110/160 degrees, diverging lilac score bars with mono dot-product readouts that rise/fall live and highlight the leading key, cream on-object labels, all colors via CSS var tokens, reduced-motion rule, no props required.
